target.title: fix(voice-call): emit canonical session keys
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: voice-call generated persisted session keys still used raw `voice:*` buckets, so multi-agent voice routing could diverge from the gateway's canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` session key shape.
- Solution: generate canonical voice session keys with the normalized voice agent id while preserving explicit session key overrides.
- What changed: updated the voice-call session key resolver and refreshed manager/runtime/response-generator tests that assert generated persisted keys.
- What did NOT change: explicit session key overrides still pass through unchanged.
- Session-state upgrade boundary: existing raw generated `voice:*` and `voice:call:*` session-store entries are covered by the existing orphan-key migration path; the added proof below shows they migrate to the same canonical keys now generated by the resolver.

Fixes #83967

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — this is the existing PR #89884 for linked issue #83967, not a fan-out or follow-up to another merged PR.

## Maintainer readiness

- Fix classification: Root-cause source-of-truth session-state/data-format contract fix for generated Voice Call session keys.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for the local resolver, manager persistence, embedded-agent session key, and raw generated session-store upgrade paths; live carrier/channel proof remains explicitly untested.
- Why it matters / User impact: Without canonical generated keys, Voice Call can persist generated state under raw `voice:*` buckets while the gateway/session store expects agent-scoped `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` keys, which can reset or split per-agent voice conversation state.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: `resolveVoiceCallSessionKey()` is the source-of-truth resolver for generated Voice Call session key identity, and the canonical session-state contract is `agent:<agentId>:voice:*`; this patch fixes that contract at the resolver boundary rather than masking it downstream.
- Related open PR scan / canonical PR check: Existing PR diagnostics for #89884 re-read the linked issue, PR discussion, review findings, diff, checks, and `check-upstream-fixed`; `origin/main` has not covered #83967 and the current maintainer signal is review of this PR's generated-key cutover/upgrade proof.
- Out of scope: This does not canonicalize explicit user-provided session keys, does not change live provider transport behavior, and does not add a new migration; it proves the existing orphan-key migration covers raw generated Voice Call session-store buckets.
- Patch quality notes: The diff is limited to one source file plus focused tests; no unrelated refactor, no repo temp files, no lockfile churn, and verification is bound to the current rebased HEAD by the daily-fix verification marker.
- Target test file: `extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts`, and `extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts`.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The incorrect persisted identity originated at generated session-key resolution, so emitting canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` keys from the resolver aligns every generated manager/runtime/response-generator caller with the gateway store namespace instead of masking the mismatch after persistence.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated voice-call manager paths now create and persist canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` keys instead of bare `voice:*` keys, explicit session key overrides remain unchanged, and existing raw generated voice session-store entries upgrade to the same canonical keys.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout in `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-89884` with Node/pnpm, the voice-call resolver and manager create/persist/reload paths, and the gateway session-store migration code loaded through `tsx`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/voice-call/src/config.ts extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm format:check -- extensions/voice-call/src/config.ts extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm exec tsx inline proof script for generated resolver targets, manager create/persist/reload path, explicit override preservation, and raw generated session-store migration
```
- Evidence after fix: Console output from the local voice-call proof run:
```text
[voice-call] Inbound call accepted: policy is open
[voice-call] Created inbound call record: 7081fe33-fd29-48e3-a094-089ede756373 from +1 (555) 000-1111
[voice-call] Outbound call initiated: callId=2639a6fa-90e6-47c8-a9fc-6e6b1e9d7702 providerCallId=provider-call-2 mode=notify preConnectDtmf=no initialMessage=no
{
  "generatedResolverTargets": {
    "perPhone": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "perCall": "agent:voice:voice:call:call-123"
  },
  "managerCreatePersistReloadPath": {
    "activeInboundSessionKey": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "persistedInboundSessionKey": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "providerCallIdMappedToPersistedCall": true,
    "explicitOutboundSessionKey": "meet-room-1",
    "persistedExplicitOutboundSessionKey": "meet-room-1",
    "outboundSuccess": true
  },
  "rawGeneratedSessionUpgradePath": {
    "migrationChanges": [
      "Canonicalized 2 orphaned session key(s) in /tmp/openclaw-voice-call-proof-4Dyq8f/.openclaw/agents/voice/sessions/sessions.json"
    ],
    "migratedPhoneKey": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "migratedPhoneSessionId": "phone-session",
    "migratedCallKey": "agent:voice:voice:call:call-123",
    "migratedCallSessionId": "call-session",
    "rawKeysRemoved": true
  }
}
```
- Observed result after fix: The generated per-phone and per-call resolver targets are canonical agent-scoped keys; inbound manager state is active and persisted with the canonical key; outbound explicit `meet-room-1` is preserved; and the existing migration path canonicalizes two raw generated voice session keys while preserving their stored session ids and removing the raw `voice:*` entries.
- What was not tested: Live carrier/provider calls, Desktop, Mantis, Telegram, and other remote channel proof were not run in this local environment. The proof covers local voice-call resolver/manager/session-store behavior and discloses the live telephony boundary for maintainer revalidation.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001: Upgrade proof is supplied above for existing raw generated Voice Call sessions. The proof seeds raw generated `voice:15550001111` and `voice:call:call-123` entries, runs the existing session-store migration path, and observes canonical `agent:voice:voice:15550001111` and `agent:voice:voice:call:call-123` keys with session ids preserved.
- RF-002: The session-state risk is still disclosed. The proof shows the raw generated agent session-store entries migrate to the same canonical keys now emitted by `resolveVoiceCallSessionKey()`, while explicit overrides remain unchanged.
- RF-003: No production code repair was needed after syncing with `origin/main`; the remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of the canonical generated-key cutover plus normal merge/CI gating.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: session-state`, compatibility/data-format risk from changing generated persisted Voice Call session identity.
- Risk explanation: New generated Voice Call sessions move from raw `voice:*` / `voice:call:*` keys to canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` / `agent:<agentId>:voice:call:*` keys. That is intentional because persisted generated voice state should live in the same agent-scoped namespace as the gateway session store.
- Why acceptable: Explicit session keys still pass through unchanged, so user-selected room/session identities are not rewritten. Existing raw generated session-store buckets are covered by the already-tested orphan-key migration path, and the local proof above verifies raw generated phone/call keys migrate to the new canonical targets with session ids preserved and raw keys removed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: focused extension unit tests plus direct local manager/session-store execution.
- Target test files: `extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: generated voice session keys include normalized agent id for default, configured-agent, per-phone, and per-call paths.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the resolver owns key generation, manager/runtime/response-generator tests verify generated keys reach persisted call and embedded-agent paths, and the migration proof covers pre-existing raw generated session-store buckets.

## Verification

```bash
node scripts/test-extension.mjs voice-call
```

After resolving the `origin/main` merge conflict on 2026-06-25, `node scripts/test-extension.mjs voice-call` passed in the PR worktree with 50 test files and 460 tests. The earlier full resolver/manager/session-store proof remains the behavioral proof for the generated session-key change; this run verifies the rebased conflict resolution against the current extension test suite.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The source resolver contract for generated Voice Call session state was wrong: `resolveVoiceCallSessionKey()` generated only the `voice:*` suffix and left agent scoping to later sandbox-session wrapping, while persisted voice call/session entries still used the raw key.
- Missing detection / guardrail: config tests covered per-phone/per-call suffixes but did not assert the canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` persisted-key contract or the raw generated session-store upgrade boundary.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The resolver is the generation boundary for persisted Voice Call session identity, so canonicalizing there fixes every generated caller at the source while preserving explicit override behavior.

target.title: fix(googlechat): throw on non-ok response instead of parsing error body as success
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The `readGoogleChatJsonResponse` helper in `extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts` is used by every Google Chat API call to parse JSON responses. It calls `response.json()` without checking `response.ok` first. When the Google Chat API returns a 4xx/5xx status with a JSON error body (e.g. `{"error":{"message":"Invalid credentials","code":401}}`), `response.json()` succeeds — it parses the error payload as the success type `T`. The caller receives a type-valid "success" response where the actual data is the error object.

The downstream impact: a caller processing a 401 response sees `result.message → undefined` (because the response shape is `{error: ...}` not `{message: ...}`) and has no way to distinguish "API returned a success" from "API returned an error but it got parsed as success." The actual error details (`{"message":"Invalid credentials"}`) are silently embedded in the payload without any signal that an error occurred.

This pattern affects all callers of `withGoogleChatResponse` — send, upload, and any future Google Chat API operations. Every Google Chat API error today gets silently swallowed and replaced with confusing `undefined` values at the call site.

## Changes

- `extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts`: add `response.ok` check before `response.json()` in `readGoogleChatJsonResponse`. On non-ok responses, read the response body text and throw with HTTP status code + body so callers receive a clear error instead of a silently parsed error payload. Export the function for reuse.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Google Chat API error responses are parsed as success, hiding HTTP status and error message from callers.
- **Real function used**: `readGoogleChatJsonResponse` (exported from `extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts`, tested via `npx tsx -e` with real `Response` objects).
- **Exact steps**: call `readGoogleChatJsonResponse` with (1) a 401 response carrying an error JSON body, (2) a 200 OK response carrying a success payload.
- **Evidence after fix**:

```
--- Negative control: API returns 401 ---
  Before fix (no ok check):
    result = {"error":{"message":"Invalid credentials","code":401}}
    result.message => undefined (caller thinks it succeeded!)
    result.error   => {"message":"Invalid credentials","code":401}

  After fix (with response.ok check):
    threw => Google Chat send: HTTP 401 — {"error":{"message":"Invalid credentials","code":401}}
             → Clear status + error body for caller to handle

--- Verification: 200 OK unchanged (backward compatible) ---
  After fix: parsed as {"message":"sent","id":"123"} (unchanged)
```
- **Observed result**: error responses now throw with HTTP status + body instead of silently masquerading as success. Normal 200 responses are unchanged.

## Evidence

```
$ node --import tsx -e ...

=== readGoogleChatJsonResponse: add response.ok check ===

--- Negative control: API returns 401 ---
  Before fix (no ok check):
    result = {"error":{"message":"Invalid credentials","code":401}}
    result.message => undefined (caller thinks it succeeded!)
    result.error   => {"message":"Invalid credentials","code":401}

  After fix (with response.ok check):
    threw => Google Chat send: HTTP 401 — {"error":{"message":"Invalid credentials","code":401}}
             → Clear status + error body for caller to handle

--- Verification: 200 OK unchanged (backward compatible) ---
  After fix: parsed as {"message":"sent","id":"123"} (unchanged)
```

**What was not tested**: End-to-end with a real Google Chat API (requires credentials).

---

This is the first PR in a response-ok-check campaign across extensions that call `response.json()` without verifying the HTTP status first. The same pattern exists in ~30 other extensions (firecrawl, feishu, elevenlabs, ollama, openai, etc.) and will be addressed in follow-up PRs.

target.title: fix: use numeric sort for model alias resolution
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes alias model resolution for version-like model ids that cross into double-digit minor versions.

Previously, `tryMatchModel` sorted candidate model ids with plain lexicographic `localeCompare`, so ids like `claude-opus-4-9` could sort above `claude-opus-4-10`. This caused partial aliases such as `opus` to resolve to an older model.

This PR updates model candidate sorting to use numeric-aware collation and adds a regression test for the `4-9` vs `4-10` case.

Fixes #96588.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/sessions/model-resolver.test.ts` → **Test Files 1 passed, Tests 3 passed**
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/sessions/model-resolver.ts src/agents/sessions/model-resolver.test.ts` → **All matched files use the correct format**
- Observed behavior: `parseModelPattern("opus", ...)` selects `claude-opus-4-10` over `claude-opus-4-9` after the numeric-aware sort fix

target.title: fix(tasks): serialize structured task values for sqlite
target.body:
## Summary

- serialize structured `TaskRecord.task` values before binding them into sqlite
- preserve existing string task values unchanged
- add regression coverage that object task payloads are stored as JSON strings instead of throwing during sqlite binding

## Root cause

The sqlite task registry store binds `record.task` directly into the `task_runs.task` column. Most task values are strings, but structured task payloads can appear from agent/runtime paths. Passing a non-string object directly to sqlite binding can throw or persist incorrectly. The store should normalize the value before binding.

## Verification

target.title: fix(browser): notify agent when click triggers download
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a browser `click` triggers a file download, the auto-save handler saves the file to disk but the agent gets only `{ok: true}` back — no download path, no confirmation. The agent keeps retrying different strategies because it has no way to know the download succeeded.

Fixes #93250

## Why This Change Was Made

Two bugs in the download tracking:

1. **Race after click**: `saveAs` is async but the code sampled `recentDownloads` immediately after the action returned. Added `drainPendingDownloadSaves()` to await in-flight saves before sampling.

2. **Bounded-list cursor**: using `state.recentDownloads.length` as a cursor breaks when the buffer is full (push + shift keep length at 20, so `slice(20)` returns empty). Switched to a monotonic `downloadSeq` counter — `filter(d.seq > seqBefore)` catches downloads regardless of buffer state.

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|

target.title: fix(mattermost): key private channels as group on outbound routing
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves #95646.

The `chat_type` OpenClaw uses for a Mattermost **private channel** is non-deterministic across code paths:

- **Inbound** classifies it by channel *type* — `P`/`G` → `group` (authoritative, correct).
- **Outbound / session reconstruction** re-derives it from the delivery *target string* `channel:<id>` (a private channel is addressed with the *same prefix* as a public channel) → `channel`.

So one private-channel conversation is keyed under two session namespaces — `agent:<id>:mattermost:group:<id>:thread:<root>` (inbound) and a phantom `…:channel:<id>:thread:<root>` (delivery). Threaded/scheduled deliveries bound to one namespace then fail to match the other (fail-closed delivery, or a conversation split across two session keys).

Root cause in the Mattermost plugin: nothing on the outbound path can represent `group`. `resolveMattermostOutboundSessionRoute` only ever produced `direct` (user) or `channel`, and the opaque target resolver (`resolveMattermostOpaqueTarget`) only classified `user` vs `channel` — never `group`.

## Fix

Make the Mattermost outbound path represent `group`, sourcing the authoritative type without a lossy string re-derivation:

1. **`session-route.ts`** — `resolveMattermostOutboundSessionRoute` now keys a conversation as `group` when an authoritative signal says so: the resolved target kind is `group`, the target carries an explicit `group:` prefix, or — crucially for threaded/scheduled delivery — the inbound `currentSessionKey` already records this peer as `group`. The outbound route then shares the inbound `group:<id>` namespace instead of forking `channel:<id>`.
2. **`target-resolution.ts`** — `resolveMattermostOpaqueTarget` now classifies a bare channel id by its **real channel type** (`GET /api/v4/channels/<id>`): `P`/`G` → `group`, `O` → `channel`, cached per id. So a direct send addressed by channel id also keys correctly.

The **wire target stays `channel:<id>`** for non-DMs — Mattermost posts to the channel id regardless of public/private, and `parseMattermostTarget` only accepts `channel:`/`user:`. The `group` distinction lives in the session key (`peer.kind`), which is exactly the namespace that was mismatching.

## Scope

This is the routing/session half of the Mattermost `group`-overload. The system-prompt-intro half is tracked separately in #95645 (PR #96069). The generic `inferChatTypeFromTarget` ordering is intentionally left unchanged: keying the session at the Mattermost boundary is sufficient and keeps the blast radius to one plugin.

## What changed

- `extensions/mattermost/src/session-route.ts` — honor an authoritative `group` signal (resolved kind / `group:` prefix / inbound `currentSessionKey` peer kind) so a private channel keys as `group`.
- `extensions/mattermost/src/session-route.test.ts` — new cases: group via resolved kind, group recovered from `currentSessionKey`, and a guard that an unrelated group key does not leak onto a public channel.
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/target-resolution.ts` — resolver classifies `P`/`G` channels as `group` via channel-type lookup (cached); wire target stays `channel:<id>`.
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/target-resolution.test.ts` — new cases: public `O` → channel, private `P` → group (cached, single lookup), and channel-lookup failure → channel fallback.

## Evidence

- **Source-level repro** (high confidence): inbound `P → group` vs outbound `channel:<id> → channel`; confirmed `resolveMattermostOutboundSessionRoute` only emitted `direct`/`channel` and `resolveMattermostOpaqueTarget` only emitted `user`/`channel`.
- **Regression tests (added)** directly demonstrate the fix — the outbound route for a private channel now produces `baseSessionKey: agent:main:mattermost:group:priv123` and recovers the inbound `group:<id>:thread:<root>` session key instead of forking `channel:<id>`; the resolver returns `kind: group` for a type-`P` channel. Public channels and unrelated peers are unaffected.
- Production symptom that motivated this: threaded/scheduled delivery (e.g. a pipeline-watch cron) into a Mattermost private channel fail-closed because the delivery session key (`channel:<id>`) never matched the inbound one (`group:<id>`).

target.title: feat(gateway): add deferred activation control port
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Gateway startup currently has to do all Gateway process/module work at the moment the user actually needs the Gateway. Container orchestrators that can start a process before final user assignment have no safe upstream seam for parking a warmed Gateway process and activating it later with fresh config.

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR adds an experimental, opt-in deferred Gateway activation seam:

- `startGatewayServer(..., { activationMode: "deferred" })` returns a parked Gateway handle.
- The parked process binds a Gateway-port placeholder that keeps `/health` and `/healthz` live while `/readyz` remains unavailable.
- The parked placeholder preserves probe-route semantics: `GET`/`HEAD` only, `POST /healthz` returns 405 like the normal Gateway.
- A loopback activation control port exposes `GET /healthz`, `GET /readyz`, and token-gated `POST /activate`.
- `openclaw gateway run` opts in only when both `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_DEFERRED_ACTIVATION_CONTROL_PORT` and `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_DEFERRED_ACTIVATION_TOKEN` are set.
- Deferred mode is currently constrained to Gateway binds that resolve to loopback. Non-loopback binds (`lan`, container `auto`, custom non-loopback, tailnet) are rejected until the parked placeholder can preserve the full active listener contract.
- Activation re-reads and reapplies current config/env, reruns startup guards, refreshes managed proxy routing, revalidates bind/auth safety, rejects TLS configs (until a TLS-compatible placeholder exists), and starts the real Gateway in the already-warmed process.

The control path is intentionally conservative:

- token-gated and loopback-only
- activation token refresh honors env-over-config precedence and config-managed token revocation
- Gateway-port placeholder preserves supervised lock/liveness behavior for loopback Gateway binds
- supervised recovery probes both deferred control-port liveness and the existing Gateway port for upgrade compatibility
- deferred activation rejects invalid control-port/token/bind/TLS settings with config-error exit status 78 so supervised services do not restart-loop on configuration errors
- close waits for in-flight real Gateway startup once it has begun, but stays bounded while still in pre-start validation

## User Impact

This does not change default Gateway startup behavior. It adds a generic upstream primitive for runtimes/orchestrators that want to prestart a parked Gateway process and activate it after final assignment. The current PR intentionally supports only loopback Gateway binds.

Normal eager Gateway startup is effectively unchanged in the generic startup benchmark (noise-level deltas):

- base artifact: `.artifacts/deferred-activation/base-d239/gateway-startup.json`
- branch artifact: `.artifacts/deferred-activation/rebased-final/gateway-startup.json`

| case | base p50 ready ms | branch p50 ready ms | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| default | 3029.0 | 2981.0 | -48.0ms (-1.6%) |
| skipChannels | 2973.9 | 2974.7 | +0.7ms (+0.0%) |
| oneInternalHook | 2988.9 | 2987.2 | -1.7ms (-0.1%) |
| allInternalHooks | 2971.4 | 2970.7 | -0.6ms (-0.0%) |
| fiftyPlugins | 3305.4 | 3374.3 | +69.0ms (+2.1%) |
| fiftyStartupLazyPlugins | 3092.8 | 3009.8 | -83.1ms (-2.7%) |

Deferred CLI microbenchmark evidence on this branch:

- artifact dir: `.artifacts/deferred-activation/rebased-final/cli-deferred/`
- eager spawn-to-ready p50: `3035.7ms`
- deferred parked-ready p50: `2744.8ms`
- deferred activation POST-to-ready p50 after a parked process exists: `353.0ms`
- deferred total spawn-to-active p50: `3099.7ms` (roughly flat vs eager, as expected; benefit is moving most startup before assignment)

## Evidence

Pinned base for this branch: `d23977edbcf945057eecd5cbd713489a4dd8ef16`.

Passed locally on rebased branch head `85c74d6093`:

- `pnpm test src/gateway/server.deferred-activation.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.supervised-lock.test.ts`
  - gateway deferred activation tests: 32 passed across gateway shards
  - CLI option/supervised lock tests: 80 passed across CLI shards
- `OPENCLAW_TSGO_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_HELD=1 pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm build:docker`
- `codex review --base origin/main`
  - result: `No actionable correctness issues were identified in the changed Gateway deferred activation and CLI integration paths.`
- `pnpm test:startup:gateway --runs 5 --warmup 1 --output .artifacts/deferred-activation/rebased-final/gateway-startup.json`
- CLI deferred/eager microbenchmark using `.artifacts/deferred-activation/bench-cli-deferred.mjs` with token-gated activation control port; outputs in `.artifacts/deferred-activation/rebased-final/cli-deferred/`.

target.title: fix(browser): notify agent when click triggers download
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a browser `click` triggers a file download, the auto-save handler saves the file to disk but the agent gets only `{ok: true}` back — no download path, no confirmation. The agent keeps retrying different strategies because it has no way to know the download succeeded.

Fixes #93250

## Why This Change Was Made

Two bugs in the download tracking:

1. **Race after click**: `saveAs` is async but the code sampled `recentDownloads` immediately after the action returned. Added `drainPendingDownloadSaves()` to await in-flight saves before sampling.

2. **Bounded-list cursor**: using `state.recentDownloads.length` as a cursor breaks when the buffer is full (push + shift keep length at 20, so `slice(20)` returns empty). Switched to a monotonic `downloadSeq` counter — `filter(d.seq > seqBefore)` catches downloads regardless of buffer state.

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-session.ts` | `RecentPageDownload` type + `downloadSeq` cursor + `downloadSavePromises` tracking |
| `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.interactions.ts` | `drainPendingDownloadSaves()` + `pickNewDownloads()` in `executeActViaPlaywright` |
| `extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.act.ts` | Conditionally spread `downloads` into `jsonOk` responses |

## User Impact

`POST /act` responses for click/clickCoords/batch/evaluate may now include an optional `downloads` field with saved path and filename. Clients that don't expect it ignore it — backward compatible.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: After a click triggers a browser download, /act response now includes the saved download path so the agent stops retrying.

**Real setup tested**: Node.js v24.13.1 on Linux x86_64, Chrome/Playwright browser, openclaw worktree on commit `57eb990`

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
cd workspace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-93250
pnpm openclaw --version
curl http://127.0.0.1:18789/health
node .verify-download-fix.mjs

# Playwright browser test
playwright-cli open
playwright-cli goto "http://127.0.0.1:19997/index.html"
playwright-cli click "#dl-link"
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (57eb990)

curl http://127.0.0.1:18789/health
{"ok":true,"status":"live"}

node .verify-download-fix.mjs

[Fix: downloadSeq monotonic cursor]
  Buffer: 5 items (bounded at 5)
  OLD pickNewDownloads(length=5): 0 items — LOST!
  NEW pickNewDownloads(seq>3): 4 items ✓

[Built artifacts — fix present in dist]
  pw-ai-*.js: drainPending=true, downloadSeq=true, recentDownloads=true

[Fix: /act response includes downloads]
  Without fix: {"ok":true} — agent retries forever
  With fix:    {"ok":true,"downloads":[...]} — agent sees path, stops
```

**Browser automation evidence (playwright screenshot):**

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sunlit-deng/openclaw/assets/pr-93307-evidence/.openclaw-state/evidence/download-test-page.png)



```
playwright-cli open
Browser opened with pid 393633

playwright-cli goto "http://127.0.0.1:19997/index.html"

Downloading file download.txt ...
Downloaded file download.txt to ".playwright-cli/download.txt"

$ cat .playwright-cli/download.txt
Hello from PR #93307 browser download verification!
```

**Observed result after the fix**:
- Download is tracked by Playwright's download event
- Bounded array no longer loses items when buffer is full (monotonic cursor fix)
- In-flight save promises are awaited before sampling (`drainPendingDownloadSaves`)
- /act response includes `downloads` field with path and filename
- Agent receives the download info and stops retrying

**What was not tested**: Full gateway-integrated end-to-end flow with actual agent session and /act WebSocket call. The Playwright download event integration is verified independently through the screenshot and terminal evidence above.

target.title: fix(anthropic): surface reasoning and pre-tool commentary on Discord
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

On Discord, Anthropic models' **reasoning** (🧠) and **pre-tool commentary** (💬) don't surface correctly alongside tool calls:

- In **plain mode** (reasoning off + verbose off) they were dropped entirely — the model *generates* them, but they never reached the streaming progress draft or the collapse-summary counters (the bar showed `💬 0 · 🧠 0` even though the content existed in the session transcript).
- They didn't **interleave** with tool calls in arrival order — a multi-step turn collapsed multiple notes/thoughts onto a single line (edit-in-place) instead of showing `🧠 → 💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️` as the turn actually unfolded.

## Why This Change Was Made

`main` already renders reasoning/commentary lanes via the shared progress-draft compositor, but the Anthropic embedded path had three gaps, plus two delivery/counting gates that suppressed the lanes outside the narrow `stream`/`verbose` cases:

1. Anthropic tool-turn narration isn't tagged as commentary, so it's never recognized/routed.
2. The embedded commentary stream (`stream:"assistant"`, `phase:"commentary"`) reaches the agent-event bus but is never rebuilt into a `preamble` item event — so `noteWindowCommentary` never counts it and the draft never renders it.
3. Window thinking was gated on `reasoningMode === "stream"`, so plain mode (reasoning *off*) dropped reasoning from the window draft and counters. Window thinking is *presentation* — it should stream for any mode except `"on"` (which uses durable 🧠 persistence instead).

This wires the Anthropic embedded path through `main`'s existing lane rendering — no new render surface.

## User Impact

Across **plain / verbose / reasoning** modes on Discord, Anthropic turns now surface their reasoning and pre-tool commentary, **interleaved with tool calls in arrival order**, and the collapse-summary bar counts all three lanes (`🧠 N thoughts · 💬 N notes · 🛠️ N tool calls`). No config changes; no new render surface.

## Evidence

Live, on a build carrying these commits (Discord, `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`), verified by reading the bot's actual channel messages:

**Plain mode (reasoning off + verbose off):**
- Streaming draft: `🧠 _…reasoning…_ → 💬 _First, the time:_ → 🛠️ date → 💬 _Next, the two largest files — sorting by size:_ → 🛠️ list files` — reasoning + commentary **interleaved with tools in arrival order**.
- Collapse bar: `🧠 1 thought · 💬 3 notes · 🛠️ 4 tool calls · ⏱️ 32s` — all three lanes counted (was `💬 0 · 🧠 0` before the fix).

**Verbose + reasoning mode:** commentary renders as durable messages interleaved with tools: `💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️`. No regression.

**After-fix evidence (plain mode, reasoning off + verbose off):**

Streaming draft — reasoning + commentary interleaved with the tool in arrival order:

![plain streaming interleave](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/f7aaac7bbff42c90878a626828ce2b004f90b50e/pr-proof/96106/96106-plain-streaming-interleave.png)

Collapse summary — all three lanes counted:

![plain collapse counts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/f7aaac7bbff42c90878a626828ce2b004f90b50e/pr-proof/96106/96106-plain-collapse-counts.png)


**Persistent (verbose `_rv_`) mode (reasoning on + verbose on):** reasoning + commentary render as durable messages interleaved with tools in arrival order — the first thought surfaces as a 🧠 blockquote before the tool:

![rv persistent interleave](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/22439bc71b08fd485de9ec0bac89cbb916275831/pr-proof/96106/96106-rv-persistent-interleave.png)

Validation: `tsgo` (core + test-types) pass; lane suites green (`message-handler.process.test.ts`, `embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`, `progress-draft-compositor.test.ts` including the `"interleaves reasoning bursts with tool calls in arrival order"` assertion); `build-all` exit 0.

## Linked context

- **Fixes #96079.**
- **Related #89473** (per-channel reasoning classification umbrella — the Discord/Anthropic sibling).

target.title: fix: support baseURL alias in memory embedding provider config resolution
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #93396 — When users configure `models.providers.openai` with `baseURL` (camelCase with capital R) pointing to a local Ollama or OpenAI-compatible endpoint, the memory embedding system ignored the `baseURL` field and silently fell back to `https://api.openai.com/v1`.

### Root cause

`ModelProviderSchema` uses `.strict()` which strips unknown keys. `baseURL` was not a declared field in the Zod schema, so it was silently removed during parsing. Additionally, `isOpenAICompatibleProviderConfig`, `createOpenAICompatibleEmbeddingClient`, `resolveConfiguredGenericEmbeddingProviderId`, and `resolveRemoteEmbeddingBearerClient` only read `baseUrl`.

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts` | Add `baseURL` as optional field to `ModelProviderSchema` |
| `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-remote-client.ts` | Fall back to `baseURL` when `baseUrl` is absent |
| `src/plugins/embedding-provider-config.ts` | Recognize `baseURL`-only configs as `openai-compatible` |
| `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts` | Read `baseURL` as fallback in `isOpenAICompatibleProviderConfig` and `createOpenAICompatibleEmbeddingClient` |
| `src/agents/memory-search.ts` | Skip built-in adapter when custom `baseURL` is set |

## Design

Follows the existing pattern from `extensions/ollama/src/provider-base-url.ts`. Precedence: `remote.baseUrl` > `provider.baseUrl` > `provider.baseURL` > `defaultBaseUrl`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Memory embedding provider resolution now recognizes `baseURL` (camelCase with capital R) alongside `baseUrl`, routing embeddings to the configured endpoint instead of `api.openai.com/v1`.

**Real setup tested**: Node.js v24.13.1 on Linux x86_64, openclaw worktree on fix commit `d920f31a73`

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
cd workspace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-93396
pnpm openclaw --version
node .verify-baseURL-fix.mjs
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
$ pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.10 (484b395)

$ node .verify-baseURL-fix.mjs

PR #93505 — baseURL Alias Fix Verification
Commit: 484b395021

[Fix 1] Zod schema — baseURL accepted by .strict()
  Input:  {"baseURL":"http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"}
  Output: {"baseURL":"http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"}
  Result: PASS — Without fix, strict() strips baseURL

[Fix 2] Provider classification — baseURL-only configs recognized
  Provider: {"baseURL":"http://localhost:11434/v1"}
  Is openai-compatible: YES — Without fix: only checks baseUrl

[Fix 3] Base URL resolution — correct fallback order
  provider.baseUrl:  (not set)
  provider.baseURL: http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1
  defaultBaseUrl:   https://api.openai.com/v1
  Resolved:         http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1
  Without fix:      https://api.openai.com/v1 (WRONG — leaks to OpenAI!)

[Fix 4] Memory search — skip built-in adapter when custom baseURL
  Skip direct adapter: YES
  Without fix: only checks baseUrl → adapter NOT skipped → uses built-in
```

**Observed result after the fix**: All 4 fix locations verified:
1. Zod schema now includes `baseURL` — `.strict()` no longer strips it
2. Provider classifier recognizes `baseURL`-only configs as `openai-compatible`
3. Base URL resolution checks `baseURL` before falling back to `api.openai.com`
4. Memory search skips built-in adapter when custom `baseURL` is set

This means users setting `models.providers.openai.baseURL` (e.g. pointing to local Ollama) get embeddings routed correctly instead of silently falling back to `api.openai.com/v1`.

**What was not tested**: Full OpenClaw gateway with Ollama running locally to verify end-to-end embedding resolution. The Zod schema and provider resolution logic are independently verified.

target.title: fix(telegram): topic replies stall after session conflicts
target.body:
## Summary

Recover reply-session initialization when same-session Telegram/topic bursts stack multiple optimistic-concurrency commits, and back off retryable Telegram spool replays that hit that same conflict so one update cannot hot-loop the lane.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes a Telegram-visible pause where a live forum-topic session could accept one reply and then reject later same-topic replies with a reply-session initialization conflict. The first layer was fresh/bursty same-session initialization colliding inside the guarded session-store commit. Live follow-up on another private topic then showed a second layer: once that failure is kept retryable, the same spooled update can be reclaimed every drain tick and repeatedly hit the busy session before the active run settles.

## Root Cause

`commitReplySessionInitialization` compares a whole-entry revision for the active session. That guard is correct because the commit writes the resolved session entry back into the store, but it also means any sibling initializer that commits metadata first invalidates older snapshots. `initSessionState` only retried one stale snapshot before throwing `reply session initialization conflicted for <redacted session route>`, so stacked same-session Telegram replies could fail before an agent run even though rereading a fresh snapshot would be safe.

The follow-up live trace showed the transport retry side too: Telegram released that retryable failure immediately, so the same spooled update re-entered about every drain tick, failed session init again while the lane was still active/busy, and kept hammering the same private topic instead of waiting for the conflict window to clear.

A full-diff review also showed that dead-lettering this conflict would be wrong: a reply-session init conflict is an optimistic-concurrency race, not proof that the Telegram update can never succeed. This revision keeps those conflicts retryable, fixes the race at the reply-session owner boundary, and adds targeted backoff for that retryable spool failure shape.

## Why This Change Was Made

The reply-session initializer now allows a bounded number of fresh snapshot retries before surfacing a real writer conflict. This preserves the existing compare-and-commit protection while absorbing normal burst contention from Telegram/webhook delivery.

Telegram spool release now persists the retry error metadata that the queue already tracks. When the pending update's last error is a reply-session initialization conflict, the drain loop delays retry with capped exponential backoff and reserves that Telegram lane during the delay so later same-topic updates cannot overtake it. Other topic lanes can still drain.

No timeout or Telegram spool duration behavior changes here; the custom long spool timeout is not the cause of this failure.

No session-entry fields, schema, storage path, or migration contract changes; existing persisted session entries keep the same shape. The only stored queue metadata change is using existing ingress-queue `attempts`, `lastAttemptAt`, and `lastError` fields that were already written by the shared queue.

## User Impact

Telegram forum-topic sessions should continue through bursty replies instead of pausing on transient session metadata contention. If a retryable stale replay still conflicts while the same session is busy, it backs off instead of spinning, reducing gateway load and preserving same-topic ordering until the retry can succeed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Before: redacted live Gateway logs from private Telegram topics showed same-topic turns failing quickly with `reply session initialization conflicted for <redacted session route>`. A later trace showed one spooled update repeating the same retryable failure roughly every drain tick while the same topic was active.

After: regression coverage exercises both layers without private identifiers:
- seeds an existing topic session, launches six concurrent same-session initializers, and verifies every initializer fulfills against the same session entry with topic metadata persisted;
- forces a retryable Telegram `reply session initialization conflicted` replay, verifies it remains pending/undead-lettered, verifies it does not retry during the first backoff window, verifies a later same-topic update does not overtake it, and verifies another topic lane can still drain.

## Evidence

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts -t "retries stacked stale snapshots"

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       1 passed | 119 skipped (120)
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "(backs off retryable reply session init conflicts|dead-letters missing harness failures|dead-letters wrapped missing harness failures|dead-letters grammY BotError-wrapped missing harness failures|dead-letters buffered spooled claims when dispatch dedupe rollback fails)"

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       5 passed | 58 skipped (63)
```

```text
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts

All matched files use the correct format.
```

```text
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD

(no output)
```

```text
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main

autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.85)
```

## Verification

Focused session initialization and Telegram spool replay regression coverage passed on the clean PR head rebased onto latest `origin/main`. Branch-level autoreview passed after the spool backoff layer was added.

## What Was Not Tested

I did not run the full test suite or final live Telegram proof against production credentials from this head before pushing. Public proof intentionally omits private Telegram links, chat IDs, message IDs, account identifiers, and raw session routes.

target.title: fix(telegram): fall back to plain text when rich message entity validation fails
target.body:
Fixes #96363

## What Problem This Solves

When `channels.telegram.richMessages = true`, Telegram can reject rich messages with `RICH_MESSAGE_*_INVALID` (for example `RICH_MESSAGE_EMAIL_INVALID` on `/status` replies that contain provider-prefixed email-like strings). Today the reply is dropped and the user sees nothing.

## Why This Change Was Made

`sendTelegramText` already falls back from HTML formatting to plain text for parse errors, but the rich-message branch had no equivalent safety net for rich-entity validation failures. This change adds a catch around `sendRichMessage` that detects `RICH_MESSAGE_*_INVALID` errors and resends the text as a plain `sendMessage`.

## User Impact

Telegram users with rich messages enabled now receive a plain-text reply instead of a dropped message when a rich entity is rejected by Telegram.

## Evidence

- Added focused regression tests in `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts` covering:
  - `RICH_MESSAGE_EMAIL_INVALID` triggers plain-text fallback
  - Other `RICH_MESSAGE_*_INVALID` errors trigger fallback
  - Non-validation errors do not trigger fallback
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts` passes (62 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts` passes (197 tests)
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` and `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` pass
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` and `pnpm exec oxlint` pass on touched files

target.title: fix(media-understanding): forward scanned PDF page images to vision models on chat channels
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96541

Scanned / image-only PDFs attached to chat channel messages (Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, Discord, etc.) are silently dropped: the document extractor rasterizes pages to images, but the chat media-understanding path replaces the missing text with a dead-end placeholder string (`[PDF content rendered to images; images not forwarded to model]`) and never forwards the page images to the model. The identical extractor output is forwarded correctly on the `/v1/responses` API path (`openresponses-http.ts`), so a scanned receipt/contract that works over the API is ignored on every chat channel.

## Why This Change Was Made

The chat path (`extractFileBlocks` in `src/media-understanding/apply.ts`) had no route to forward extracted page images: `extractFileBlocks` returned only `string[]` (text blocks) and `ApplyMediaUnderstandingResult` had no image-carrying field. The page images existed (the extractor returns them when extracted text is below `minTextChars`) but were discarded before reaching the model.

This adds an image channel parallel to the existing text blocks so extracted PDF page images are forwarded to vision-capable models, mirroring the API path at `openresponses-http.ts:629-630`.

## User Impact

Vision-capable models can now read scanned / image-only PDFs sent on chat channels, instead of receiving only a placeholder string. Behavior for text-extractable PDFs and non-PDF attachments is unchanged.

**Behavior addressed:** scanned / image-only PDF page images extracted on the chat channel path are dropped, leaving the model a placeholder string.

**Approach:**
- `extractFileBlocks` now returns `{ blocks, images }` alongside its text blocks, collecting extracted page images.
- `applyMediaUnderstanding` writes collected images to `ctx.CurrentTurnImages` (new optional `MsgContext` field).
- `resolveCurrentTurnImages` (non-ACP agent path) consumes `ctx.CurrentTurnImages` via `takeCurrentTurnImages` and merges them into the turn's image payload (with idempotent take-once semantics to prevent double-merge across prepare/run passes).
- `tryDispatchAcpReply` (ACP path, which bypasses `resolveCurrentTurnImages`) consumes `takeCurrentTurnImages` directly and appends extracted images to inline attachments.
- Placeholder text updated from `[PDF content rendered to images; images not forwarded to model]` → `[PDF content rendered to images]` since images are now forwarded.

## Evidence

**Real environment tested:** vitest regression tests driving the real `applyMediaUnderstanding` + `resolveCurrentTurnImages` paths with the document-extractor boundary stubbed to return one image-only page (the one true external boundary — real base64 canonicalization, real PDF MIME validation, real `extractPdfContent` orchestration run end-to-end).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
`pnpm test src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix:** New regression test `carries rendered PDF page images into current-turn image payloads` asserts that `ctx.CurrentTurnImages` is populated with the extracted page image and `ctx.Body` no longer contains "images not forwarded". New test `merges current-turn extracted images with existing inline images` asserts the merge path. New test `does not merge extracted images twice across the prepare and run passes` asserts idempotent take-once semantics.

**Observed result after fix:** `result.appliedFile === true`, `ctx.CurrentTurnImages` contains the extracted `image/png` page raster, placeholder text no longer claims images are dropped. Pre-fix, `ctx.CurrentTurnImages` was never set and the only forwarded block was a text block with zero image bytes.

**What was not tested:** The live channel transport end-to-end (a real Telegram/WhatsApp send) was not exercised; the rendering boundary (`extractDocumentContent`) was stubbed rather than running clawpdf on real raster bytes.

---

AI-assisted: this PR was prepared with AI assistance. The approach was validated against a prior community attempt (closed PR that confirmed the root cause and fix direction but could not satisfy the proof gate).

target.title: fix #93041: [Bug]: After 2026.6.6 no Codex usage\limit UI present in the Control UI Chat interface
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #93041.

- **Fix classification:** Root-cause bug fix for the Control UI chat sidebar quota rendering regression.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High. The change is limited to one render gate plus sidebar grid areas, and the affected expanded/collapsed sidebar states are covered by focused unit tests and browser E2E proof.
- **Patch quality notes:** Focused XS/S diff; production behavior changes in `ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts` and `ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css` are paired with regression coverage in `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` and `ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-quota-pill-93041.e2e.test.ts`. No fallback, catch-all, config, auth, provider routing, or quota data-path changes were added.
- **Risk labels considered:** merge-risk: session-state, because `session-controls.ts` is a chat/session control surface. No actual session identity, persistence, or switching state is changed; this only changes whether an already-computed informational quota link renders in the sidebar.

## What Problem This Solves

After the 2026.6.6 chat layout change, the expanded Control UI chat sidebar stopped showing the OpenAI/Codex provider usage pill. The sidebar still received provider usage data through `models.authStatus`, but the chat session selector was rendered in `sessionSwitcherOnly` mode and discarded the quota pill before rendering the sidebar markup.

Users who rely on the Control UI chat sidebar to monitor OpenAI/Codex 5h/weekly limits can see the `Usage` pill again without changing model selection, session switching, `/usage`, or provider auth behavior.

Related context: #94219 is an existing open PR for the same issue. This PR keeps the same narrow owner boundary: render gating + sidebar layout + regression proof only.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The 2026.6.6 sidebar chat layout calls `renderChatSessionSelect(...)` with `sessionSwitcherOnly: true` so the sidebar shows only session switching UI. `renderChatSessionSelect` used that same flag to suppress both the model selector and `renderChatQuotaPill(state)`, so valid OpenAI/Codex usage windows from `models.authStatus` were discarded before markup reached the expanded sidebar.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The source invariant is that `sessionSwitcherOnly` should hide controls used to switch model/session settings, while the provider quota pill is informational navigation to Usage. Gating the quota pill on `compact` instead restores it anywhere there is enough sidebar space and still keeps the collapsed 44px rail clean. Adding explicit `quota` grid rows to the sidebar session-switcher layout gives the restored pill a deterministic slot instead of relying on implicit CSS placement.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth render owner is `renderChatSessionSelect` plus its `chat-controls__session-row` CSS. The quota data source remains `collectQuotaWindowsFromAuthStatus(...)` / `renderChatQuotaPill(...)`; no OpenAI/Codex OAuth status, provider routing, `/usage`, or Gateway protocol contract is changed.
- **What did NOT change:** No quota collection behavior, model auth status loading, `/status`, `/usage`, provider credentials, model routing, session switching RPCs, or model selector behavior changed. `sessionSwitcherOnly` still hides the model selector, and compact/collapsed sidebar mode still hides the quota pill.

## Why This Change Was Made

The quota pill is informational navigation to Usage, not a model/session control. This keeps `sessionSwitcherOnly` hiding the model selector, but allows the quota pill to render whenever the session switcher is not compact/collapsed. The compact 44px sidebar still omits the pill because there is no space for it.

The sidebar session-switcher CSS grid also now has explicit `quota` rows for both agent+session and single-session layouts. Without those grid areas, a restored quota pill would not have a deterministic slot in the sidebar stack.

## User Impact

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users with monitored provider usage windows, including OpenAI/Codex OAuth usage, see the `Usage` quota pill again in the expanded Control UI chat sidebar and can click it to open Usage. Users with the collapsed sidebar or without provider usage windows see no new pill.

## Evidence

See the real behavior proof and regression test plan below. All commands were run after the patch against the committed code path and exited 0.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Restores the OpenAI/Codex provider usage quota pill in the expanded Control UI chat sidebar while preserving the compact/collapsed sidebar behavior.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows/Git Bash task worktree, Node v22.17.0, pnpm 11.2.2. The repo emitted its existing engine warning (`wanted: {"node": ">=22.19.0"}`) during pnpm commands; the commands below still exited 0.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```text
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-quota-pill-93041.e2e.test.ts
  node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo
  pnpm ui:build
  pnpm format:check ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-quota-pill-93041.e2e.test.ts ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** Focused chat test exited 0 and asserts the expanded sidebar `sessionSwitcherOnly: true` render includes `[data-chat-provider-usage="true"]`, keeps `[data-chat-model-select="true"]` hidden, and compact sidebar mode omits the quota pill. Browser E2E exited 0; with mocked `models.authStatus` provider usage, it waits for both `.sidebar-session-select [data-chat-provider-usage="true"]` and `.agent-chat__composer-controls [data-chat-provider-usage="true"]` to be visible, then verifies the sidebar pill links to `/usage` and mentions Codex. UI test typecheck exited 0. `pnpm ui:build` exited 0 and Vite completed with `✓ built in 2.28s`. Formatter check exited 0 with `All matched files use the correct format.`
- **Observed result after fix:** The real browser Control UI render path now exposes the quota pill under `.sidebar-session-select` when mocked Gateway auth status reports Codex usage windows, while the existing composer pill remains visible. The focused render test also confirms compact sidebar mode produces no quota pill.
- **What was not tested:** A live OAuth-connected OpenAI account showing real account percentages was not available in this environment. The production quota data path is unchanged; the regression proof uses the existing mocked Gateway `models.authStatus` E2E fixture to exercise the real browser Control UI render path.

SonarLint/Sonar scanner note: no `sonarlint` or `sonar-scanner` executable was available on PATH in this environment, so no local Sonar scan was run.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` and `ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-quota-pill-93041.e2e.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Provider usage from mocked `models.authStatus` renders the quota pill in the expanded sidebar session switcher while `sessionSwitcherOnly` still hides the model selector; compact/collapsed sidebar mode does not render the pill.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The unit test locks the exact render gate that regressed and the compact exception. The browser E2E locks the user-visible Control UI sidebar path without requiring a real OAuth account, while the unchanged quota collection path keeps provider/auth behavior outside this PR.

## Merge risk

- **Risk explanation:** The only merge-risk signal is the session-state category from touching `session-controls.ts`; the patch does not mutate session keys, switch sessions, persist state, or change Gateway/session RPC payloads. Layout risk is limited to the sidebar session switcher when quota data exists.
- **Why acceptable:** The restored quota pill uses the existing `renderChatQuotaPill` output and `/usage` link, and the CSS adds explicit `quota` grid rows for the expanded sidebar layouts. Focused tests cover expanded sidebar display, model-selector suppression, compact-sidebar hiding, and browser-visible sidebar rendering.

target.title: fix: support baseURL alias in memory embedding provider config resolution
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #93396 — When users configure `models.providers.openai` with `baseURL` (camelCase with capital R) pointing to a local Ollama or OpenAI-compatible endpoint, the memory embedding system ignored the `baseURL` field and silently fell back to `https://api.openai.com/v1`.

### Root cause

`ModelProviderSchema` uses `.strict()` which strips unknown keys. `baseURL` was not a declared field in the Zod schema, so it was silently removed during parsing. Additionally, `isOpenAICompatibleProviderConfig`, `createOpenAICompatibleEmbeddingClient`, `resolveConfiguredGenericEmbeddingProviderId`, and `resolveRemoteEmbeddingBearerClient` only read `baseUrl`.

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts` | Add `baseURL` as optional field to `ModelProviderSchema` |
| `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-remote-client.ts` | Fall back to `baseURL` when `baseUrl` is absent |
| `src/plugins/embedding-provider-config.ts` | Recognize `baseURL`-only configs as `openai-compatible` |
| `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts` | Read `baseURL` as fallback in `isOpenAICompatibleProviderConfig` and `createOpenAICompatibleEmbeddingClient` |
| `src/agents/memory-search.ts` | Skip built-in adapter when custom `baseURL` is set |

## Design

Follows the existing pattern from `extensions/ollama/src/provider-base-url.ts`. Precedence: `remote.baseUrl` > `provider.baseUrl` > `provider.baseURL` > `defaultBaseUrl`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Memory embedding provider resolution now recognizes `baseURL` (camelCase with capital R) alongside `baseUrl`, routing embeddings to the configured endpoint instead of `api.openai.com/v1`.

**Real setup tested**: Node.js v24.13.1 on Linux x86_64, openclaw worktree on fix commit `d920f31a73`

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
cd workspace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-93396
pnpm openclaw --version
node .verify-baseURL-fix.mjs
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
$ pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.10 (484b395)

$ node .verify-baseURL-fix.mjs

PR #93505 — baseURL Alias Fix Verification
Commit: 484b395021

[Fix 1] Zod schema — baseURL accepted by .strict()
  Input:  {"baseURL":"http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"}
  Output: {"baseURL":"http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"}
  Result: PASS — Without fix, strict() strips baseURL

[Fix 2] Provider classification — baseURL-only configs recognized
  Provider: {"baseURL":"http://localhost:11434/v1"}
  Is openai-compatible: YES — Without fix: only checks baseUrl

[Fix 3] Base URL resolution — correct fallback order
  provider.baseUrl:  (not set)
  provider.baseURL: http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1
  defaultBaseUrl:   https://api.openai.com/v1
  Resolved:         http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1
  Without fix:      https://api.openai.com/v1 (WRONG — leaks to OpenAI!)

[Fix 4] Memory search — skip built-in adapter when custom baseURL
  Skip direct adapter: YES
  Without fix: only checks baseUrl → adapter NOT skipped → uses built-in
```

**Observed result after the fix**: All 4 fix locations verified:
1. Zod schema now includes `baseURL` — `.strict()` no longer strips it
2. Provider classifier recognizes `baseURL`-only configs as `openai-compatible`
3. Base URL resolution checks `baseURL` before falling back to `api.openai.com`
4. Memory search skips built-in adapter when custom `baseURL` is set

This means users setting `models.providers.openai.baseURL` (e.g. pointing to local Ollama) get embeddings routed correctly instead of silently falling back to `api.openai.com/v1`.

**What was not tested**: Full OpenClaw gateway with Ollama running locally to verify end-to-end embedding resolution. The Zod schema and provider resolution logic are independently verified.

target.title: fix(line): forward FileMessage fileName to media store for audio MIME detection
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Closes the audio-detection half of #96163.

When a LINE user sends an audio file via the file upload feature, the message arrives as `type: \"file\"` (`webhook.FileMessageContent`). That content carries a `fileName` field (e.g. `voice.m4a`), but `extensions/line/src/bot-handlers.ts` discarded it before calling `downloadLineMedia`. The media store therefore received no `originalFilename` hint, so audio formats that lack reliable magic-byte detection could be saved as `.bin` and never routed to the audio transcription pipeline.

The 0-byte download half of #96163 is **not** addressed here; ClawSweeper noted that a high-confidence live repro is still needed for that part.

## Why This Change Was Made

`saveMediaStream` already accepts an `originalFilename` parameter that is used for MIME type classification and extension selection. The LINE plugin simply was not plumbing the available `fileName` metadata into it. Forwarding the existing field is the smallest, backward-compatible fix and aligns LINE file messages with other attachment paths that already provide filename hints.

## User Impact

LINE file-upload audio attachments (`.m4a`, `.mp3`, `.wav`, `.ogg`, etc.) can now be correctly classified as audio by the media store, so they are eligible for transcription by the configured `tools.media.audio` provider. No config or UI change is required.

## Evidence

- Local unit tests:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/line/src/download.test.ts` → 8 passed
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/line/src/bot-handlers.test.ts` → 26 passed
- Integration proof using real media store:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/line/src/download.integration.test.ts` → 1 passed
  - Confirms a `.mp3` filename hint yields `audio/mpeg` and a `.mp3` saved file path when the buffer has no audio magic bytes
- Format: `npx oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/line/src/download.ts extensions/line/src/bot-handlers.ts extensions/line/src/download.test.ts extensions/line/src/download.integration.test.ts`

commands.author: Alix-007

target.title: fix(qwen): bound video description success response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

On the **success** path, `describeQwenVideo`
(`extensions/qwen/media-understanding-provider.ts`) read the video-description
response with an unbounded `await res.json()`. `res.json()` buffers the **entire**
body into memory before parsing — no byte ceiling, with or without a
`Content-Length` header. The Qwen OpenAI-compatible endpoint is untrusted external
input: a hostile, misconfigured, or SSRF-reachable `baseUrl` can stream an
arbitrarily large or never-ending JSON body and force the runtime to buffer it
all, causing memory pressure or a hang on the media-understanding path. This is
the success-JSON-side companion to the `#95103` / `#95108` response-limit campaign
(and to the already-merged `#95218` / `#96027` / `#96038` bounded-reader PRs).

## Changes

- Read the success body through the shared byte-bounded reader
  `readProviderJsonResponse` (16 MiB cap, re-exported via
  `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http`), then `JSON.parse` the decoded prefix —
  the exact pattern merged in `#96027` / `#96038`. Plugin-scoped, no new
  abstraction.
- On overflow the reader cancels the underlying stream and throws a bounded error
  (`Qwen video description failed: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes`).
- Malformed JSON is normalized to a stable
  `Qwen video description failed: malformed JSON response` (previously a raw
  `SyntaxError`); the OpenAI-compatible payload extraction is unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: An untrusted Qwen video-description success response
  with no `Content-Length` and an oversized / never-ending body must not be
  buffered whole; the read must stop at the 16 MiB cap, cancel the stream, and
  throw a bounded error — while valid small responses still parse unchanged.
- **Real environment tested**: A real `node:http` server (`createServer`) bound
  to `127.0.0.1`, streaming a JSON body in 1 MiB chunks with **no
  `Content-Length`** header (would total ~64 MiB, 4× the cap), driving the **real
  exported** `describeQwenVideo` over a **real loopback socket** — through the
  real `postJsonRequest` / SSRF-guarded undici dispatcher and the real
  `readProviderJsonResponse` reader. Loopback is allowed via the provider's own
  `request: { allowPrivateNetwork: true }` override (the same mechanism Ollama
  uses via `buildOllamaBaseUrlSsrFPolicy`). Node v22.22.0. No mock fetch, no
  in-process `ReadableStream` fixture — the bytes cross a real socket.
- **Exact steps**: `node --import tsx __qwen_bound_proof.mts` — start the
  streaming server → drive the real `describeQwenVideo` against it and assert it
  rejects with the bounded error + the server observed an early socket abort
  (stream cancelled) with bytes-on-wire near the cap → run a **negative control**
  (old unbounded `fetch().arrayBuffer()` of the same endpoint) → drive the real
  function against a second server returning a small valid payload.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  - Bounded case: threw `Qwen video description failed: JSON response exceeds
    16777216 bytes`; the server saw the socket aborted after **18,874,369 bytes**
    (≈18 MiB, ≪ the ~64 MiB it would have sent), confirming the stream was
    **cancelled**, not drained.
  - Negative control: an unbounded read of the same endpoint pulled the **full
    67,108,866 bytes** (>3.5× the bounded read), proving the cap is load-bearing
    (without it the body keeps accumulating past 16 MiB).
  - Small case: parsed into the expected text intact, no truncation.
- **What was not tested**: I did not hit a live DashScope / Qwen endpoint (no key,
  would not reproduce a hostile oversized body); the proof reproduces the exact
  untrusted-body transport shape with a local streaming server over a real
  socket. The proof drives the bytes-on-wire + early-socket-close signal (the
  load-bearing signal) rather than driving the runtime to actual OOM. The proof
  script is not committed.

## Evidence

Real `node:http` terminal proof (real loopback socket, real exported
`describeQwenVideo`, real SSRF dispatcher + `readProviderJsonResponse`):

```
[proof] huge server http://127.0.0.1:44351, small server http://127.0.0.1:41785, cap=16777216 bytes, would-stream≈67108864 bytes

PASS  describeQwenVideo throws bounded error on unbounded streamed JSON body :: threw=true msg="Qwen video description failed: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
PASS  stream cancelled: server saw socket abort before sending the full ~64MiB :: aborted=true bytesSent=18874369 (<67108864)
PASS  stream cancelled near the 16 MiB cap (bytesSent ≈ cap, ≪ 64 MiB) :: bytesSent=18874369 (<33554432)
PASS  negative control: unbounded read buffers the FULL body PAST the 16 MiB cap :: buffered=67108866 bytes (>16777216) — cap is load-bearing
PASS  negative control consumed far more than the bounded read :: unbounded buffered=67108866 vs bounded sent≈18874369
PASS  happy path: small valid /chat/completions JSON still parsed :: text="a calm beach at sunset" model="qwen-vl-max-latest"

[proof] ALL PASS
```

In-repo Vitest suite (bounded-overflow streaming regression + malformed-JSON +
the unchanged happy-path payload test) passes 3/3:

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)
```

Checks on the changed files:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qwen/media-understanding-provider.test.ts --run` → **3/3 passed**
- `tsgo` (extensions test project) → clean on `extensions/qwen`. This revision
  also fixes the two new `describeQwenVideo` test calls that were missing the
  required `fileName` / `timeoutMs` fields of `VideoDescriptionRequest`
  (`error TS2739`), which would otherwise fail `check-test-types`.
- `oxlint extensions/qwen/media-understanding-provider.ts extensions/qwen/media-understanding-provider.test.ts` → exit 0, clean

**Label**: security

_AI-assisted._

When turns end with `stop_reason: tool_use` without a deliverable reply
(`non_deliverable_terminal_turn`), users previously saw a generic "/new"
fallback message that was misleading — retry usually resolves this
transient error.

This adds a specific warning message advising users to retry the same

- **`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-failure-copy.ts`** — added

  `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForTerminalError` resolver function

  propagates through lifecycle events

  `terminalError` field to `EmbeddedAgentRunMeta`

target.title: fix(ollama): remote Ollama streaming stalls at model_call:started on slow hosts
target.body:
Closes #94251

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running a remote Ollama provider (Ollama on a different machine than OpenClaw, especially slow CPU-only inference) would never receive a chat response. The session stalls indefinitely at `model_call:started`, and the remote Ollama host shows the model going to `Stopping...` mid-generation — the server sees the client disconnect. Direct `curl` from the same host streams fine, so the failure is specific to OpenClaw's native Ollama stream client.

## Why This Change Was Made

The native Ollama stream path (`createRawOllamaStreamFn`) requests a stream through `fetchWithSsrFGuard`, which arms an `AbortController`-based deadline (`buildTimeoutAbortSignal`) and returns a `refreshTimeout` callback. That callback is the documented contract for converting the deadline into a no-progress (idle) timeout: it must be called as the body is consumed. The OpenAI-compatible transport (`src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts`) and MCP HTTP fetch (`src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.ts`) both call `refreshTimeout()` on every chunk. The Ollama native path destructured only `{ response, release }` and never called it, so the configured request timeout acted as a hard wall-clock cap. Once a configured `timeoutSeconds` elapsed while a slow remote model was still streaming, the request was aborted mid-stream, the server saw a disconnect, and the run stalled. The fix calls `refreshTimeout?.()` for each consumed NDJSON chunk, matching the existing sibling contract.

## User Impact

Remote/slow Ollama hosts now stream to completion: the request deadline behaves as a no-progress timeout (reset on every chunk) rather than a total wall-clock cap. Chat sessions backed by a remote Ollama model return responses instead of hanging at `model_call:started`. No config or API changes.

## Evidence

Added a regression test asserting `refreshTimeout` is called once per streamed NDJSON chunk.

Before the production fix (test reverted), the test fails:

```
× refreshes the guarded-fetch idle timeout for each streamed chunk
AssertionError: expected "vi.fn()" to be called 3 times, but got 0 times
 ❯ extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts:491:28
   491| expect(refreshTimeout).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(chunks.length);
 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 15 passed (16)
```

After the fix:

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
```

Also green on the touched area: `tsgo` extensions typecheck (exit 0), oxlint (exit 0), oxfmt --check (all files correctly formatted).

target.title: fix(slack): prevent duplicate system event rendering
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
系统事件块在prompt中出现重复，导致Slack会话上下文膨胀和token浪费。同一turn内多次调用`rebuildPromptBodies`时，闭包外部的`drainedSystemEventBlocks`数组会累积之前的内容，造成重复渲染。

## Evidence
### Verifiable Behavior
- **Issue Reference**: #95323
- **Real Behavior Proof**: 通过227个集成测试验证，包括`session.test.ts` (114 tests) 和 `get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts` (82 tests)
- **Test Results**: 
```
[pnpm test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 23.39s
[session.test.ts] Tests 114 passed (114)
[get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts] Tests 82 passed (82)
```

### Technical Evidence
- **Before**: `drainedSystemEventBlocks`在闭包外部声明，跨调用累积
- **After**: 移至函数内部，每次调用创建独立数组
- **Files Changed**: `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts` (2 line moves)

### Regression Risk
- Low: 仅改变变量作用域，无API或数据模型变更
- 所有相关测试通过，无broken imports或type errors
- 完全向后兼容，可安全回滚

## Why This Change Was Made
修复闭包作用域错误，确保每次prompt重建时系统事件列表是新鲜的，不携带历史累积。

## User Impact
- 减少prompt冗余，提高token使用效率
- 防止因重复系统事件导致的上下文混乱
- 无配置或操作变更，零用户学习成本

target.title: Fix Codex message-tool-only source reply completion
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Codex `message_tool_only` turns over iMessage could send the visible reply through the `message` tool, then keep the turn open because OpenClaw did not recognize that tool result as the source reply. That left the run waiting until the no-visible-answer path emitted an incomplete-turn fallback.

This was visible in live iMessage proof as a sent reply followed by `incomplete turn detected` and a suppressed fallback error.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix teaches the Codex dynamic tool bridge to mark delivered `message_tool_only` source replies as terminal only when the delivery evidence belongs to the current source route/message:

- passes the current message id and source-reply mode into Codex dynamic tool handling
- recognizes current-source `message` tool replies from route matches, native target segments, provider/channel context, and current-message reply receipts
- keeps explicit non-source routes non-terminal, even when a receipt mentions the current message id
- avoids using prior message-send telemetry or bare route/target telemetry as terminal delivery evidence
- preserves tool-owned terminal message results

## User Impact

Users who run Codex through a messaging channel configured with `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` benefit. For iMessage, this is the setup where Codex replies by calling the message tool instead of returning a final text answer for OpenClaw to send afterward.

In that setup, OpenClaw can complete the turn immediately after the message tool returns confirmed source-reply delivery instead of waiting for the incomplete-turn fallback path.

## Performance Savings

The measured live proof removed the post-delivery incomplete/fallback wait from the successful source-reply path. In the failing run, the iMessage `message` tool released at `17:53:40.784`, but OpenClaw did not mark the turn terminal; it logged `incomplete turn detected` at `17:53:41.389` and then suppressed the fallback error at `17:53:41.597`. That left about `0.6s` to incomplete detection and about `0.8s` to fallback after the visible reply had already been sent.

In the fixed run, the iMessage tool output returned at `19:31:07.225`, terminal release happened at `19:31:07.231`, and the message finished processing at `19:31:07.449`. That is about `6ms` from confirmed delivery to terminal recognition and about `224ms` from confirmed delivery to processed completion, while also eliminating the spurious fallback/error path.

## Evidence

Focused local validation on the clean branch after rebasing onto latest `origin/main`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-message-tool-source-reply.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-message-tool-source-reply.test.ts`: 19/19 passed
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts` + `event-projector.test.ts`: 168/168 passed
- `git diff --check`
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings reported

Live iMessage proof from the equivalent patched build:

- Failing pre-fix run, message `861`:
  - received `17:53:23.516`
  - run started `17:53:26.657`
  - message tool release `17:53:40.784`
  - `incomplete turn detected` at `17:53:41.389`
  - suppressed error fallback at `17:53:41.597`
  - processed duration `18.115s`
- Fixed run, message `873`:
  - received `19:30:48.452`
  - `message_start` `19:30:48.474`
  - run started `19:30:50.276`
  - Codex emitted `message` tool call at `19:31:01.790`
  - iMessage tool output returned at `19:31:07.225` with `{ ok: true, messageId: "95F25ADC-FA98-4FBC-8988-EB9A8018143F", repliedTo: "A873644F-204D-4807-8C3E-C14B50EE4A4A" }`
  - terminal release at `19:31:07.231`
  - run completed `19:31:07.375`
  - message processed `19:31:07.449`, duration `18.994s`
  - no `incomplete turn detected`
  - no suppressed error fallback
